summary: Kenya is a global leader in mobile money, anchored by Safaricom's M-Pesa platform, which has over 30 million active users. M-Pesa enables person-to-person transfers, bill payments, savings, and loans via mobile phone without requiring a bank account. The government has integrated M-Pesa and other mobile money platforms for tax collection (iTax), government-to-person payments, and social protection disbursements. The Central Bank of Kenya regulates mobile money under the National Payment System Act.

sources.title: Central Bank of Kenya - National Payment System

notes: M-Pesa launched in 2007 and now processes transactions equivalent to approximately 50% of Kenya's GDP. Mobile money has been transformative for financial inclusion, with over 80% of adults using mobile money services. Kenya's success has served as a model for mobile money deployments across Africa and beyond.
